jobText:
Job description
We are seeking a talented individual to join our Career Consulting team at Mercer as a Summer Intern Analyst. This role will be based in Washington DC. This is a hybrid role that has a requirement of working at least three days a week in the office. Internship program dates are Monday, June 1st ŌĆō Friday, August 7th, 2026.

Mercer is uniquely qualified to help organizations design and execute sophisticated workforce strategies, whether itŌĆÖs for a multinational organization or a local company attempting to grow within its market. We provide best-in-class consulting, rigorous analytics, and data-driven insights that help organizations make fact-based decisions.

As a Summer Intern Analyst on MercerŌĆÖs Career team, you will support client engagements across industries as an integral member of project teams. YouŌĆÖll help diagnose client challenges and contribute to the design of strategic talent solutions across Rewards, HR Transformation, Talent Strategy, Workforce Planning & Analytics, and Communications. The role combines hands-on research, modeling, and problem solving with client interaction, working with senior clients and internal experts in a collaborative, learning-oriented environment. You will gain practical exposure to MercerŌĆÖs integrated approach to improving business performance through people.

We will count on you to:
ŌĆó Conduct research and analysis to understand industry and organization-specific issues that could include business strategy, corporate performance, compensation and rewards practices and trends, global expansion, and best practices in talent management
ŌĆó Collect and examine data relevant to developing solutions tailored to the clientŌĆÖs needs
ŌĆó Analyze clients' strategic, financial, and organizational information to provide insights for more senior members of the project team
ŌĆó Participate in team brainstorming sessions to develop client recommendations
ŌĆó Prepare client presentations, including recommendations to be presented to boards of directors, executives, senior management, and HR leaders

What you need to have:
ŌĆó Progress toward a bachelor's degree
ŌĆó Status as a currently enrolled student; candidates should have an expected graduation date between the Spring of 2027 through Spring of 2028
ŌĆó Strong academic achievement demonstrated through rigorous course work, and the ability to successfully balance extra-curricular activities
ŌĆó A strong interest in human capital consulting
ŌĆó Legal work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis without the need for sponsorship now or in the future is required

What makes you stand out?
ŌĆó Strong analytical skills, both quantitative and qualitative, and working knowledge of Microsoft OfficeŌĆö specifically Excel and PowerPoint
ŌĆó Knowledge of data analysis, storytelling with data, and presentation design
ŌĆó Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills
ŌĆó Intellectual curiosity; seeking opportunities to develop new skills and apply critical thinking and innovative solutions to solve problems
ŌĆó Ability to collaborate with diverse teams and communicate with others in a skillful, engaging, and proactive manner
ŌĆó Strong time management skills, with the flexibility to work under tight deadlines and adapt to changing client needs
ŌĆó Superior organizational skills and strong attention to detail

##
ŌĆó * Current Employees:
ŌĆó * If you are a current Staff, Faculty or Temporary employee at the University of Miami, please click to log in to Workday to use the internal application process. To learn how to apply for a faculty or staff position using the Career worklet, please review this .The UHealth system at the University of Miami has an exciting opportunity for a full time Senior Analyst, Finance Transformation - Process in the UHealth Finance Department.
ŌĆó * SUMMARY
ŌĆó * The Senior Analyst, Finance Transformation - Process - Central (H) supports the execution of the overall finance process transformation for UHealth/MSOM through project management, performance improvement initiatives, and strategic communication. The Senior Analyst, Finance Transformation ŌĆō Process ŌĆō Central (H) enables efficient and effective finance operations by planning, implementing, and tracking a variety of projects and initiatives to ensure their successful development, completion, and sustainability.

Moreover, the incumbent holds responsibility for evaluating current processes and making recommendations for improvement, while ensuring a solid communication strategy to keep all stakeholders involved and informed.
ŌĆó * CORE JOB FUNCTIONS
ŌĆó ** Works with various Finance teams to identify areas of opportunity and define the scope of projects/initiatives designed to improve finance effectiveness and efficiency at UHealth/MSOM.
ŌĆó Discusses the objectives and measures upon which projects/initiatives will be evaluated at completion and determines the resources required for execution.
ŌĆó Establishes and documents comprehensive plans and timelines which identify and sequence the activities needed to successfully complete projects/initiatives.
ŌĆó Monitors the progress of projects/initiatives and adjusts scope/strategy/timelines as needed to adapt to changes to ensure successful completion.
ŌĆó Consults with the appropriate stakeholders on the selection of employees to assist with projects/initiatives, provides training and orientation, and assigns tasks as appropriate.
ŌĆó Reviews the quality of work completed with the project team on a regular basis to ensure that work produced meets project standards.
ŌĆó Prepares presentations on completed projects/initiatives and presents status reports to stakeholders.
ŌĆó Adheres to University and unit-level policies and procedures and safeguards University assets.
ŌĆó ** This list of duties and responsibilities is not intended to be all-inclusive and may be expanded to include other duties or responsibilities, as necessary.
ŌĆó **** CORE QUALIFICATIONS
ŌĆó **

ŌĆó * Experience:
ŌĆó * Minimum 5 years of relevant experience
ŌĆó * Knowledge, Skills, and Attitudes:
ŌĆó ** Skill in collecting, organizing, and analyzing data.
ŌĆó Ability to recognize, analyze, and solve a variety of problems.
ŌĆó Ability to exercise sound judgment in making critical decisions.
ŌĆó ** Any appropriate combination of relevant education, experience and/or certifications may be considered.
ŌĆó ** The University of Miami offers competitive salaries and a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, tuition remission and more.

jobText:
ll gain hands-on exposure to the commercial real estate industry while learning directly from top producers, leaders, and mentors. This immersive internship provides the foundational knowledge, skills, and professional network needed to build a future career in commercial real estate.

Why MatthewsÖ

Your growth and learning is our mission. Every Intern participates in MatthewsÖ University, our award-winning training program designed to transform motivated individuals into confident, knowledgeable professionals. You will be developed by (and learn directly from) top executives, leaders, and producers, gaining hands-on mentorship, structured training, and collaboration with our industry-leading teams.

At MatthewsÖ, we believe in a culture where success is earned through effort, determination, and integrity. Our interns thrive in an environment that celebrates collaboration, fuels curiosity, and inspires everyone to win together.

MatthewsÖ has made an industry-leading commitment to technology and innovation. With millions invested in proprietary, AI-driven tools that streamline marketing, client outreach, and data insights, you will gain exposure to the platforms redefining how business is done in commercial real estate.

What You Will Learn

Ģ Participate in the MatthewsÖ University training program to learn the fundamentals of commercial real estate, professional development, financial underwriting, and sales, as well as brokerage, through the lens of investment sales, leasing, and capital markets.

Ģ Observe and practice marketing and lead generation techniques, including cold calling, research, and client communications.

Ģ Gain foundational understanding of market research, property valuation, and financial underwriting/investment analysis.

Ģ Learn to utilize industry leading AI-driven platforms including client relationship management and marketing tools to manage data and performance insights.

Ģ Participate in ongoing coaching, workshops, and professional development sessions designed to enhance your skills and confidence.

Requirements

Ģ A positive attitude, team orientation, and willingness to learn

Ģ Strong work ethic, discipline, integrity, and resilience

Ģ Excellent organizational and communication skills

Ģ A driven, competitive, and entrepreneurial mindset

Ģ Currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Real Estate, or related field

Ģ Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and adapt quickly to challenges

Ģ Coachable attitude with a desire for continuous improvement and personal growth

Your Opportunity

Ģ Comprehensive, structured learning through MatthewsÖ University

Ģ Direct mentorship from Market Leaders, Managing Directors, Executives, and top producers

Ģ Exposure to real client interactions and live deal processes

Ģ Professional development and networking opportunities

Who Should Apply

If you are a motivated self-starter looking to gain hands-on experience in commercial real estate brokerage, business development, or sales, we encourage you to apply. This internship is ideal for students or recent graduates eager to develop their professional skills, expand their network, and explore a rewarding career path in one of the most dynamic industries.

I see the ACAS program as the perfect opportunity to translate my technical training into clinical impact, supporting physicians during critical procedures with both precision and empathy. Conversations with Jan David further reinforced how deeply these values shape the ACAS program. What excites me most is the opportunity to grow my career with J&J MedTech in New York City. I have spent the past four years building my academic, professional, and personal foundation here, and I am eager to continue my adult life in this city while contributing to J&JÆs mission of delivering high-quality, life-changing innovation in electrophysiology. Thank you for your consideration. I would welcome the chance to discuss how my skills and passion align with the ACAS program. I can be reached at sofia.garcia@columbia.edu or 786.631.6903. Sincerely, Sofia Garcia

Cover Letter or Other Writing Samples	December 20, 2025 Hiring Committee Universal Music Group Re: 2026 Summer Internship: Business & Legal Affairs Opportunities (NY) Dear Universal Music Group Internship Team, Universal Music Group sits at the intersection of creativity, culture, and complex business operations, which is exactly where my interests lie. What draws me to UMG is the breadth of legal and business issues handled in house. From artist agreements and clearances to marketing initiatives, digital content, and technology driven deals, these roles reflect how law operates as a strategic partner rather than a purely reactive function. I am eager to gain exposure to how Business and Legal Affairs teams balance creativity, risk, and commercial realities while supporting some of the most influential artists and catalogs in the world. Before law school, I earned a degree in Advertising, where I developed a strong foundation in brand strategy, messaging, and audience awareness. That experience shapes how I think about reputation, intellectual property, and the downstream impact of contracts and business decisions in a consumer facing industry like music. Through my law school education and internships, I have gained legal experience that required research, drafting, document review, and attention to detail, while learning how legal teams collaborate with non legal stakeholders to move projects forward efficiently and responsibly. I am particularly interested in the New York based Business and Legal Affairs roles because of their focus on artist relations, deal structuring, clearance, and cross functional collaboration. I bring strong writing and organizational skills, comfort working independently, and a genuine curiosity about how legal frameworks support the release, promotion, and protection of creative work. I am also interested in the Florida based UMLE programs where I can utilize my Spanish language proficiency and knowledge of Latin label markets. jobText:
Job description
Description:

Business and Legal Affairs interns will gain experience in:
Ģ Drafting of basic agreements, amendments, and other legal documents
Ģ Analysis of content license agreements in connection with company-mandated requirements
Ģ Analysis of various agreements for input of rights information into the departmentÆs rights management system
Ģ Review and revision of form agreements.

Qualifications:
Ģ Desired Backgrounds: Must be enrolled in a Juris Doctor program at an accredited law school and have completed at least one year
Ģ Classes or experience in copyright and contracts law preferred. Trademark law also a plus.
Ģ Desired Characteristics: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, knowledge of MS Office (Word, Excel and Outlook), strong organizational skills, ability to handle multiple tasks in fast paced environment, attention to detail and a sense of confidence and discretion; knowledge of entertainment and copyright law helpful; must be able to work both in a team environment and independently.
Ģ Must be authorized to work in the United States without visa sponsorship by NBCUniversal
Ģ Needs to be able to work on-site in New York, NY or Miami, FL
Ģ Internships at NBCUniversal are paid and do not require course credit
Ģ Deadline: Interested applicants are encouraged to apply by February 15, 2019
Ģ Summer Program Dates: June - August 2019
